title: Validate uploaded answer-sheet candidate_ids against the school's roster
business_reason: FR-ANSWER-SHEET-UPLOAD-0019 accepted ANY candidate_id in the uploaded OMR CSV. answer_sheet_policy.json requires candidate_id + school_id; a candidate_id that isn't in the school's locked roster is a data-integrity error (typo, wrong school's sheet, fabricated id) and must not silently become a scored response. Validate each uploaded candidate_id against the school's roster (students with an assigned candidate_id) — accept the known ones, reject + surface the unknown ones.

design.pure_helper: server/eval/omrIngest.ts partitionByRoster(rows, rosterSet) -> {valid, unknown}. Splits parsed rows into those whose candidate_id is in the school's roster and the unknown candidate_ids. Unit-tested.
design.ingest: ingestOmrBatch gains an OPT-IN validateAgainstRoster flag: loads the school's roster candidate_ids (students.candidate_id for the batch's school, not null), partitions, persists ONLY the valid rows, counts the unknown as invalid, sets batch_status validated (or validation_failed if none valid), and returns unknown_candidate_ids. Staff import (FR-OMR-IMPORT-0010) does NOT pass the flag, so its behavior is unchanged.
design.route_ui: POST /api/school/answer-sheets passes validateAgainstRoster: true; the school page surfaces the rejected unknown candidate_ids (DOWNSTREAM visibility per P0.10).

param_14.question: What happens if a school uploads an answer sheet for a candidate_id not in its roster?
param_14.answer: It is rejected, not scored. On upload, each candidate_id is validated against the school's locked roster (students with an assigned candidate_id for that school). Known candidate_ids are persisted as responses; unknown ones are excluded, counted as invalid, and surfaced back to the school (and in the batch's invalid count) so they can be corrected. Staff imports are unaffected (opt-in).
param_14.answer_source: answer_sheet_policy.json (candidate_id_required, school_id_required, school-scoped); students.candidate_id (assigned at roster lock, CHAIN-003); evaluation_candidate_responses
param_14.security_implications: prevents a school from injecting responses for candidate_ids outside its own roster (the roster is school-scoped); the roster is loaded server-side from the batch's school_id (not client-supplied); unknown ids are rejected fail-safe (excluded), not silently accepted.
param_14.approval_implications: none new; rejected rows simply don't enter scoring.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: cross-school candidate_id (a candidate_id belonging to another school) is rejected because the roster is scoped to this school; a per-row 'why rejected' detail panel is a follow-up.
param_14.assumptions: the roster is locked (candidate_ids assigned) before answer sheets are uploaded — the real operational order.
